tagline: A fixed-rank hunter economy is broken by one player running an infinite growth protocol.
visualizationReason: Solo Leveling is defined by control links between cosmic factions, vessel inheritance, and gate-driven dependencies; the node graph best exposes who grants power, who exploits it, and how Jinwoo rewires the network.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: If the world is a dungeon, I'll clear every floor.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_WARN]: STATIC-RANK UNIVERSE BREACHED — SINGLE NODE SCALING WITHOUT CAP

introductionSummary: Solo Leveling starts in a world where awakened hunters are permanently locked to the rank they first awaken with. The hidden System violates that law by turning Sung Jinwoo into a leveling process that never hard-caps. As factions above humanity wage a long war through vessels, Jinwoo's growth changes from local survival tool into a planetary control mechanism.

aiInsights.casual: Most hunters in Solo Leveling are stuck with the power they awakened with forever. Jinwoo is different because the System lets him gain stats and skills like an RPG character, so he eventually outgrows every normal benchmark.
aiInsights.deep: The universe runs on a fixed-capacity hunter model until the Architect's System installs a controlled exponential growth loop inside a single host. That loop functions as a compatibility wrapper for Ashborn's authority, turning Jinwoo into a continuously scaling sovereign node that can absorb hostile units, erase faction asymmetry, and collapse the old Rulers-vs-Monarchs equilibrium.

powerSystem.name: Awakening Rank Lock
powerSystem.subtitle: Static Mana Ceiling
powerSystem.loreDesc: Humans who awaken as hunters receive a fixed magical ceiling that almost never changes, defining social status and survival odds from day one.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A hard-capped class assignment model: each user gets immutable baseline stats and cannot scale through ordinary training loops.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Early raids treat Jinwoo as disposable because he is an E-rank who should never exceed baseline output.

powerSystem.name: The System
powerSystem.subtitle: Adaptive Growth Interface
powerSystem.loreDesc: A mysterious quest UI forces Jinwoo through lethal missions, rewards stat points, and grants skills as if reality became a game.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A progressive load-balancing protocol that upgrades one host via XP, quests, penalties, and deterministic reward tables.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: After the Double Dungeon incident, Jinwoo accepts daily quests and begins measurable level growth no other hunter can replicate.

powerSystem.name: Shadow Extraction
powerSystem.subtitle: Post-Combat Unit Conversion
powerSystem.loreDesc: Jinwoo can command dead enemies to rise as loyal shadows, converting battlefield losses into permanent army growth.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A recursive force-multiplication mechanic that transforms defeated hostile nodes into retained, summonable assets.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Igris and later Beru become elite shadows, proving Jinwoo can convert boss-level threats into reusable command units.

powerSystem.name: Monarch Authority
powerSystem.subtitle: Sovereign Domain Control
powerSystem.loreDesc: The Monarchs and Rulers operate as cosmic sovereigns whose powers are inherited, vessel-bound, and tied to existential war mandates.
powerSystem.systemDesc: Top-layer permission architecture where authority transfer, vessel compatibility, and dimensional throughput determine strategic dominance.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Ashborn entrusts Jinwoo with full Shadow Monarch authority, replacing temporary System scaffolding with native sovereignty.

rankings.topTierLore: Primordial entities whose decisions determine planetary survival and who can only be opposed by equivalent cosmic authority.
rankings.topTierSystem: Root-level actors with cross-dimensional privileges, capable of rewriting strategic conditions for all lower tiers.

tiers.name: National Level Hunter
tiers.loreDesc: Exceptional human vessels and super-elite hunters who can alter war outcomes at national scale.
tiers.systemDesc: High-throughput combat nodes with strategic deterrence value and exceptional durability.

tiers.name: S-Rank Hunter
tiers.loreDesc: The strongest conventional awakeners in ordinary society, often guild leaders or raid anchors.
tiers.systemDesc: Top of the standard human performance distribution before cosmic authority layers activate.

tiers.name: A-E Rank Hunters
tiers.loreDesc: Mainstream awakened workforce with strict power ceilings and large survival variance by rank.
tiers.systemDesc: Static-cap worker tiers assigned at initialization with minimal dynamic progression.

characters.name: Sung Jinwoo
characters.title: The Player / Shadow Monarch
characters.loreBio: Once the weakest E-rank hunter, Jinwoo survives the Double Dungeon and becomes the only human granted unrestricted growth through the System before inheriting Ashborn's full authority.
characters.systemBio: Primary mutable node in a fixed-cap ecosystem; accumulates stats, skills, and converted units until he becomes the highest-authority combat process.
characters.signatureMoment: Accepting Ashborn's mantle and deploying his shadow army as an autonomous strategic force.

characters.name: Cha Hae-In
characters.title: The S-Rank Blade
characters.loreBio: A top Korean S-rank whose sensitivity to mana signatures makes her both elite in combat and uniquely aware of Jinwoo's abnormal evolution.
characters.systemBio: High-precision melee specialist and anomaly detector who validates Jinwoo's nonstandard mana profile against known hunter baselines.
characters.signatureMoment: Witnessing Jinwoo dominate raid-level threats that should statistically require full guild deployment.

characters.name: Yoo Jinho
characters.title: The Logistics Anchor
characters.loreBio: A wealthy but sincere hunter who partners with Jinwoo early, funding raids and helping formalize operations while remaining fiercely loyal.
characters.systemBio: Resource and coordination support node that converts capital, legal cover, and logistics into uptime for Jinwoo's growth cycle.
characters.signatureMoment: Backing Jinwoo's independent raid path when established guild structures dismiss him.

characters.name: Go Gunhee
characters.title: The Institutional Gatekeeper
characters.loreBio: Leader of the Korean Hunters Association who recognizes Jinwoo's strategic importance and shields him from reckless political exploitation.
characters.systemBio: Policy-layer stabilizer managing risk, classification, and response escalation as Jinwoo's capabilities exceed regulatory frameworks.
characters.signatureMoment: Prioritizing alliance with Jinwoo as national survival probabilities begin to depend on a single actor.

characters.name: Baek Yoonho
characters.title: The Beast Guild Commander
characters.loreBio: A veteran guild master whose instincts let him identify Jinwoo as a destabilizing force long before public metrics catch up.
characters.systemBio: Experienced combat leader functioning as comparative benchmark for pre-System S-rank ceilings.
characters.signatureMoment: Reassessing inter-guild power assumptions after observing Jinwoo's solo raid efficiency.

characters.name: Thomas Andre
characters.title: The National-Level Titan
characters.loreBio: One of the world's strongest hunters and a Ruler vessel, Thomas embodies conventional apex power before Jinwoo surpasses global standards.
characters.systemBio: Maximum-output human combat node with vessel-layer augmentation, used as a global calibration point for threat scales.
characters.signatureMoment: His clash with Jinwoo demonstrates the gap between static apex hunters and dynamic sovereign scaling.

characters.name: Ashborn
characters.title: The Original Shadow Monarch
characters.loreBio: A former Fragment of Brilliant Light who became the Shadow Monarch and ultimately chooses Jinwoo as his successor instead of consuming him.
characters.systemBio: Legacy authority kernel whose transfer protocol turns Jinwoo from externally managed player into native sovereign instance.
characters.signatureMoment: Handing over full monarch authority after Jinwoo survives the final compatibility threshold.

characters.name: Antares
characters.title: Monarch of Destruction
characters.loreBio: The most destructive Monarch and final existential opponent, Antares pushes the world to collapse-level crisis.
characters.systemBio: Terminal destructive process with maximal offensive throughput; principal adversarial node in late-stage war conditions.
characters.signatureMoment: Forcing Jinwoo and allied powers into endgame conflict where time reset becomes the only stable escape path.

relationships.loreDesc: Ashborn chooses Jinwoo as inheritor rather than vessel overwrite, creating a rare sovereign succession.
relationships.systemDesc: Authority transfer edge converting temporary player protocol into permanent monarch privileges.

relationships.loreDesc: Jinwoo's rise culminates in a world-ending duel against the Monarch of Destruction.
relationships.systemDesc: Primary terminal conflict edge between growth-optimized sovereign and maximal destruction node.

relationships.loreDesc: Cha Hae-In becomes one of Jinwoo's most trusted field allies as his battles escalate.
relationships.systemDesc: High-trust cooperative edge linking sovereign node to elite human strike specialist.

relationships.loreDesc: Jinho provides unwavering logistical and emotional support during Jinwoo's growth arc.
relationships.systemDesc: Operational dependency edge supplying capital and coordination resources to the main progression node.

relationships.loreDesc: Go Gunhee guides Jinwoo's entry into high-stakes institutional conflict.
relationships.systemDesc: Governance-to-asset advisory edge that reduces political friction around a volatile strategic node.

relationships.loreDesc: Their confrontation tests whether national-level prestige can contain Jinwoo's rise.
relationships.systemDesc: Benchmark rivalry edge comparing static apex output versus dynamic scaling trajectory.

relationships.loreDesc: Baek shifts from caution to respect as Jinwoo repeatedly saves major operations.
relationships.systemDesc: Supportive inter-guild edge that improves coalition response capacity around Jinwoo missions.

relationships.loreDesc: Longstanding monarch hostility defines the late cosmological war.
relationships.systemDesc: Historic sovereign conflict edge inherited by Jinwoo after authority transfer.

factions.name: Korean Hunters Association
factions.loreDesc: Regulatory institution coordinating gates, raids, and hunter certification within Korea.
factions.systemDesc: Civil defense governance layer handling classification, dispatch, and loss-containment decisions.

factions.name: Korean Guild Coalition
factions.loreDesc: Major Korean guilds and hunter teams attempting to contain dungeon disasters and protect civilians.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed combat workforce with uneven power distribution and high dependency on elite nodes.

factions.name: Monarchs
factions.loreDesc: Cosmic sovereigns engineered for destruction who invade through gates and vessel warfare.
factions.systemDesc: Hostile top-tier cluster pursuing planetary wipe through overwhelming force deployment.

factions.name: Rulers
factions.loreDesc: Celestial counterforce opposing Monarch annihilation while using human vessels as proxies.
factions.systemDesc: Defensive strategic layer with indirect intervention model and selective empowerment of human assets.

factions.name: Shadow Army
factions.loreDesc: An ever-expanding legion of resurrected shadows commanded solely by Jinwoo.
factions.systemDesc: Recursive summon network converting enemy casualties into permanent force inventory.

rules.name: Awakening Sets the Baseline
rules.subtitle: Rank ceilings are normally fixed
rules.loreConsequence: Most hunters remain trapped at their initial rank, making early talent lottery-like and unforgiving.
rules.systemEquivalent: Static class initialization with negligible post-deployment vertical scaling.

rules.name: Gate Failure Causes Breakout
rules.subtitle: Dungeons punish delays
rules.loreConsequence: If raid teams fail to clear a gate in time, monsters erupt into civilian zones.
rules.systemEquivalent: Unresolved dungeon instances trigger forced external spawn events and urban security collapse.

rules.name: System Quests Are Mandatory
rules.subtitle: Ignoring tasks triggers penalties
rules.loreConsequence: Jinwoo is coerced by lethal penalties whenever he refuses or fails required System objectives.
rules.systemEquivalent: Compulsory task scheduler with punitive fail states to maintain adaptation velocity.

rules.name: Shadow Extraction Requires Victory
rules.subtitle: Only the defeated can be recruited
rules.loreConsequence: Jinwoo cannot arbitrarily summon elites; he must first survive and defeat them.
rules.systemEquivalent: Unit conversion is gated behind successful combat resolution and command authority checks.

rules.name: Cup of Reincarnation Has Finite Uses
rules.subtitle: Time reset is scarce
rules.loreConsequence: Rewinding the timeline can save humanity once more, but every use permanently drains the artifact.
rules.systemEquivalent: Global rollback tool with limited charges and irreversible resource depletion.

counterplay.mechanic: Hunters clear or suppress gate instances before breakout thresholds are met.
counterplay.loreDesc: Guild raids race the clock to stop dungeon catastrophes before cities are overrun.
counterplay.systemDesc: Local intervention workflow that interrupts hostile spawn pipeline at instance level.

counterplay.mechanic: Jinwoo accumulates XP and stat allocations that bypass normal awakening constraints.
counterplay.loreDesc: The world's rule that 'people cannot grow' is broken by one character's forced progression.
counterplay.systemDesc: Adaptive progression subroutine invalidates hard-cap hunter model for a designated host.

counterplay.mechanic: Enemy casualties are converted into Jinwoo-controlled units, reducing attrition losses.
counterplay.loreDesc: Every victory grows Jinwoo's side instead of merely surviving another battle.
counterplay.systemDesc: Combat outcomes recursively expand force inventory through defeated-unit assimilation.

counterplay.mechanic: Jinwoo's transfer from player to monarch authority exceeds vessel-tier power benchmarks.
counterplay.loreDesc: Even the strongest human elites cannot match full Shadow Monarch deployment.
counterplay.systemDesc: Authority escalation from augmented human class to sovereign permission set.

counterplay.mechanic: Rulers trigger a final rollback, giving Jinwoo a cleaner strategic state to resolve the war.
counterplay.loreDesc: When direct victory costs too much, time itself becomes humanity's emergency counter.
counterplay.systemDesc: Planet-scale checkpoint restore used to re-run endgame with improved deployment conditions.

anomalies.name: Second Awakening Event
anomalies.loreDesc: Jinwoo's post-Double-Dungeon transformation disproves the belief that hunter potential can never change.
anomalies.systemDesc: Exclusive host receives mutable-stat protocol, violating global fixed-cap assumptions.

anomalies.name: Shadow Retention After System Collapse
anomalies.ruleViolated: System interface should be required for command flow
anomalies.loreDesc: Even after the visible System dissolves, Jinwoo retains command over shadows and sovereign powers.
anomalies.systemDesc: Underlying authority kernel persists after UI decommission, indicating architecture-level integration.

anomalies.name: Memory Survivors Across Timeline Reset
anomalies.ruleViolated: Rollback should fully overwrite prior state awareness
anomalies.loreDesc: Certain high-tier beings preserve memory traces despite universal time rewind.
anomalies.systemDesc: Rollback routine shows selective immunity at higher ontological layers, implying partial state persistence.

causalEvents.name: Double Dungeon Contract
causalEvents.trigger: Jinwoo survives sacrificial trial in hidden temple
causalEvents.consequence: The System designates him as player and begins forced progression.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The near-death ritual that turns the weakest hunter into the only one with infinite growth potential.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Initialization event creating a unique mutable actor in an otherwise static-rank environment.

causalEvents.name: Red Gate and Demon Castle Progression
causalEvents.trigger: System quests escalate Jinwoo into higher-risk dungeons
causalEvents.consequence: He rapidly outpaces standard S-rank benchmarks and gains elite shadow assets.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Successive high-risk clears turn Jinwoo from anomaly into dominant raid force.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Accelerated XP pipeline and boss conversion produce nonlinear combat scaling.

causalEvents.name: Ashborn Succession
causalEvents.trigger: Jinwoo passes the final compatibility threshold
causalEvents.consequence: System scaffolding ends and true Shadow Monarch authority is inherited.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The player phase ends; Jinwoo becomes the sovereign himself.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Control transfer from training protocol to native authority runtime.

causalEvents.name: Cup of Reincarnation Final Use
causalEvents.trigger: War against Antares reaches catastrophic loss threshold
causalEvents.consequence: Timeline resets, enabling Jinwoo to neutralize threats under altered conditions.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Humanity gains one final rerun to avoid extinction.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Finite global rollback creates a new execution branch with retained high-tier strategic memory.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Jinwoo's growth considered impossible in this world?
systemQuestions.answer: Because normal awakenings lock hunters to fixed rank ceilings, while the System gives Jinwoo repeatable stat growth and skill acquisition.

systemQuestions.question: What does the System actually do beyond giving quests?
systemQuestions.answer: It acts as a conditioning framework that keeps Jinwoo alive while scaling him toward compatibility with full Shadow Monarch authority.

systemQuestions.question: How do gates make the hunter economy unstable?
systemQuestions.answer: Every unresolved gate can become a breakout disaster, forcing institutions to constantly triage finite elite manpower.

systemQuestions.question: Why are factions in Solo Leveling hard to map linearly?
systemQuestions.answer: Because authority flows through vessels, hidden cosmic actors, and changing alliances rather than clean nation-versus-nation fronts.

systemQuestions.question: What makes shadow extraction strategically broken?
systemQuestions.answer: It converts enemy losses into Jinwoo's permanent roster, so each win increases future force capacity instead of just preserving status quo.

systemQuestions.question: Why does the Cup of Reincarnation matter to the ending?
systemQuestions.answer: It provides a finite timeline reset that lets Jinwoo re-engage endgame threats with better state awareness before total collapse.
